calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-02-2009 Continued To Mar-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for and with Denise Booker.

Mr. Carberry stated that Ms. Booker has declined to comply with the Court's June 16, 2008 order and that he is willing to waive his fees if Ms. Booker will fulfill her duties. Ms. Booker stated that she had not complied with the Court's order because her siblings had received funds from bank accounts and life insurance annuities which she did not receive. She stated that she believed she should get some of that money and that is why she did not comply with the Court's order. The Court stated that she was the Successor Trustee, that she must fulfill her obligations, and that it could reduce her fees. Mr. Carberry stated that this had been going on for five years, that things had happened prior to the death, and that is why Ms. Booker feels short-changed. The Court stated that there was a $10,000 reserve. Mr. Carberry stated that was held, in part, to pay for an attorney fee request submitted by him. The Court requested that Mr. Carberry submit a declaration with an informal accounting attached as to what was just talked about, and set March 16, 2009 as the status hearing date for the filing of Mr. Carberry's declaration. Discussions about an escrow account were held. Mr. Carberry stated that this was a distribution of a reserve; the original distribution had been done previously. The Court stated that it would take the matter under submission on March 16, 2009. In addition, the Court addressed the attorney fees and surcharge. Mr. Carberry stated that he was waiving the fees. The Court stated that it would consider the surcharge anyway.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2008 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar May-07-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for Denise Booker.

Mr. Carberry stated that his client had no objections. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: B. Mann.

Off Calendar Aug-01-2007 Continued To Aug-15-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Shaun Carberry, appearing for and with Denise Booker.
Counsel states that Ms. Booker does not want to sign the accounting and therefore he has not filed it. Ms. Booker states that he has not shown her any documentation pertaining to the accounting. She also wants the accounting to go back as far as her mother estate. Counsel states the accounting is to start at the date where the previous accounting was completed. The court continued the matter to 8/15/07. Counsel is to produce documentation backing up the accounting. The dcoumentaion is to be reviewed by Ms. Booker.
JUDGE:JOHN DEARMAN, REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN, CSR #11722, CLERK:LARRISA DOTSON, BALIFF:R. LEE

Off Calendar Jul-18-2007 Continued To Aug-01-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Shaun Carberry appearing for and with Denise Booker, Trustee.
Mr. Carberry states that the accounting is ready thru 6/30/07, but that Ms. Booker refuses to sign it. Ms. Booker states that she has not heard from Mr. Carberry for six months, until yesterday and would like to have a chance to look over all accountings, because she feels the accounting is incorrect. The court told the attorney and trustee to work together and resolve this matter in order to get the accounting filed.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James McCarthy (Counsel for Lynn Collins and Patrice Booker); Shaun Carberry (Counsel for the Trustee, Denise Booker).

The Court noted at the outset that the only issue before the Court was the surcharge with respect to the rent. After argument presented by counsel, the Court found that the $35,000 surcharge was appropriate and granted the same. Mr. Carberry is to prepare the Order.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722 ; Bailiff: None; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None. Matter submitted. Matter submitted.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-28-2006 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Shaun Carberry appearing for and with Denise Booker, successor trustee. James McCarthy appearing for Lynn Collins. Patrice Booker is in court. No formal opposition filed. Patrice Booker is objecting to the rent surcharge. The question on representation for Patrice Booker addressed by Mr. McCarthy. Court is approving the preliminary distribution with a reduction of $10,000.00 to everybody without prejudice to Patrice Booker filing objections to the surcharge. She is to file and serve the written objections by 10/30. If the written objections are not filed by 10/30, Court will grant the rent surcharge. Matter is set for filing of written objections on 10/30. Mr. Carberry will prepare a new order. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Teena Paxton, CSR #1558. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Off Calendar Feb-21-2006 Continued To Mar-14-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Shaun Carberry appearing with and for trustee, Denise Booker. Kelly Booker, an heir is in court. Anne Rousseau, listing agent is in court. James McCarthy appearing for Lynn Collins. Veronica Nelson, listing agent and Patrice Booker are also in court. Mr. Carberry reported to the court that Patrice has not vacated the property. The locks have been changed and the agent has not been able to show the property. Patrice asked for two more days for her family to move. Court told her that they are in violation of the court order. court is allowing them to have two days to move by 2/23 at noon. Anything not moved will be moved by the trustee. Ms. Rousseau will continue to work with Ms. Nelson as co-listing agents. Court wants answers to all the accounting questions by 3/14. Matter is continued to 3/14. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
